methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found central Student Life academic-integrity guidance, Teaching@UW AI guidance, course-only CSE pages, and a college-specific engineering page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:washington.edu University of Washington AI data privacy approved tools Copilot generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found Teaching@UW Copilot guidance, a UW Medicine policy PDF, UW-IT Copilot service documentation, and several non-policy research/course results.

methodsAttempted.query: site:washington.edu University of Washington artificial intelligence policy teaching learning generative AI syllabus guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found Teaching@UW sample syllabus statements and AI+Teaching guidance; course pages and a newsletter result were rejected or skipped for source quality.
Followed official links from UW-IT general AI guidelines to UW-IT Copilot documentation, Graduate School research guidance, Teaching@UW guidance, and login-required UW Medicine SharePoint guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official UW discovery located central general-use, student academic-integrity, teaching, approved-tool, graduate research, and UW Medicine healthcare-setting sources. Rejected sources were course-only, college-scoped, generic academic-conduct policy without AI-specific text, login-required SharePoint guidance, recommendation-only task-force material, or repository research output.

stopConditions: Stop after official University of Washington sources support central general-use, student academic-integrity, teaching, approved-tool, graduate-research, and UW Medicine scoped healthcare claims.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim is narrower than the UW-IT general-use guideline and uses the source's University data scope.
Approved because the source directly states instructor discretion for course GenAI rules.
Approved with may-violate wording to match the Student Life source language.
Approved as teaching guidance, not a student conduct rule.
Approved because Teaching@UW directly identifies Microsoft Copilot as the only currently UW-supported generative AI tool.
Approved as sample syllabus guidance only; the claim does not treat sample language as binding policy.
Approved because the UW-IT Copilot article directly describes commercial data protection for UW users.
Approved because the source directly says UW Microsoft 365 sign-in is required to use the commercial data benefit.
Approved to preserve the source-status limitation that the Graduate School guidance is recommendations only.
Approved with research-guidance scope; the claim does not represent the recommendation as a formal research policy.
Approved with UW Medicine scope to avoid implying a broader university-wide healthcare AI rule.
Approved with clinical-care/business-operations and elevated-risk scope from COMP.308.

artifacts.title: University of Washington staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for University of Washington uses official UW sources only. Approved claims cover central GenAI general-use guidance, student academic-integrity warnings, Teaching@UW course-policy guidance, UW-IT Copilot commercial-data-protection guidance, Graduate School research guidance with its recommendations-only limitation, and UW Medicine COMP.308 healthcare-setting AI policy. Rejected sources were course-only, college-scoped, generic/non-AI-specific, login-required, recommendations-only, or repository research outputs.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
UW Graduate School research guidance is explicitly recommendations-only; staged claims preserve that limitation.
UW Medicine COMP.308 claims are scoped to UW Medicine healthcare-setting AI uses and should not be read as a general university-wide student or classroom policy.
NetID-required UW Medicine SharePoint guidance was not accessed or used as evidence.
Task-force recommendations, course-only pages, and repository research outputs were not converted into approved policy claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
